title: Control MCP connectivity via auth or other access control

posts.author: enterpriseuser (enterpriseuser)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Currently, once an admin adds the monday.com MCP app, a user can use any service to authenticate to monday.com via the OAuth flow and use monday via the mcp server. We would like to be able to control the access to mcp in some way.

One common way we’ve seen this be enforced is to control access to which third party product is authorized to perform the oauth2 authentication. For example, Atlassian supports the general product domains via toggle, and allows for adding custom domains as well.

Atlassian Support

Control Atlassian Rovo MCP server settings | Atlassian Support

Control which AI tools can connect to your Atlassian organization with Rovo MCP server. Allow or block access to keep your data secure.

What are you trying to achieve

Ultimately, we want to be able to control what services our users are using to drive their AI workflows. Internally we have tools which are “allowed” and they use our guardrails etc. In a form of “belt + suspenders” when we provide MCP capabilities, we want to ensure they enforce the organization policies we have in place.
